summary:
Agent2 identified that non-parametric tests do not assume normal distribution and provided an example with the Mann-Whitney U test suitable for unpaired comparisons lacking this assumption compared to Student’s T-tests which require normally distributed populations or large sample sizes where central limit theorem applies allowing approximate normal distributions even if population isn't truly so shaped.
To keep within your instructions while focusing only on essential information related directly back towards addressing "Which one among these options represents a NON PARAMETRIC TEST", I will refine further into concise form maintaining core elements needed per requirement stated above :

Nonparametric tests like the Mann Whitney-U don’t need data to be normally distributed; they're ideal for analyzing ordinal or skewed continuous variables between unrelated samples versus traditional methods needing bell curve shape assurance across all conditions examined here.
